result.stdout:
Description:
  Generates changelog files from the Git history by utilizing conventional commits and semantic versioning in combination with a wide flexibility by providing a broad set of configuration possibilities.

Usage:
  Git.Conventional.Cli [command] [options]

Options:
  -v, --verbose   Prints what is going on during processing. Might give you an idea why things are happening like they do.
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  changelog  Generates a changelog based on the commits.
  init       Saves the default configuration file to the current repository root.
  schema     Generates the configuration json schema file to import into your editor.
  validate   Validates the commit message in the given file. Please put the command 'gc validate --msg-file $1' into the commit-msg hook.
  version    Calculates a semantic version.

commands.payload:
Description:
  Generates changelog files from the Git history by utilizing conventional commits and semantic versioning in combination with a wide flexibility by providing a broad set of configuration possibilities.

Usage:
  Git.Conventional.Cli [command] [options]

Options:
  -v, --verbose   Prints what is going on during processing. Might give you an idea why things are happening like they do.
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  changelog  Generates a changelog based on the commits.
  init       Saves the default configuration file to the current repository root.
  schema     Generates the configuration json schema file to import into your editor.
  validate   Validates the commit message in the given file. Please put the command 'gc validate --msg-file $1' into the commit-msg hook.
  version    Calculates a semantic version.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Generates a changelog based on the commits.

Usage:
  Git.Conventional.Cli changelog [options]

Options:
  -c, --config <config>      Additional configuration files to load. They will be merged in order of occurence.
  -o, --output <output>      Where to put the output file. If none is provided the output is written to the console.
  -t, --template <template>  The template file to use for generating the changelog.
  -d, --dump <dump>          Dumps the acquired information to a json file.
  -v, --verbose              Prints what is going on during processing. Might give you an idea why things are happening like they do.
  -?, -h, --help             Show help and usage information

  -o, --output <output>      Where to put the output file. If none is provided the output is written to the console.
  -t, --template <template>  The template file to use for generating the changelog.
  -d, --dump <dump>          Dumps the acquired information to a json file.
  -v, --verbose              Prints what is going on during processing. Might give you an idea why things are happening like they do.
  -?, -h, --help             Show help and usage information

Options:
  -v, --verbose   Prints what is going on during processing. Might give you an idea why things are happening like they do.
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Generates the configuration json schema file to import into your editor.

Usage:
  Git.Conventional.Cli schema [options]

Options:
  -o, --output <output>  Where to put the output file. [default: conventional.configuration.schema.json]
  -v, --verbose          Prints what is going on during processing. Might give you an idea why things are happening like they do.
  -?, -h, --help         Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Validates the commit message in the given file. Please put the command 'gc validate --msg-file $1' into the commit-msg hook.

Usage:
  Git.Conventional.Cli validate [options]

Options:
  -m, --msg-file <msg-file>  The path to file containing the git message.
  -v, --verbose              Prints what is going on during processing. Might give you an idea why things are happening like they do.
  -?, -h, --help             Show help and usage information
